title: Industrialization and Migration Empire
description: This exercise will focus on migration and its effects during the age of imperialism using the themes of ENV, ECN, and SIO and addressing learning objectives F, G, and H. It will have 3 types based on the three LOs and themes, scaled x6 (total 12).
topic_path: Causes of Migration in an Interconnected World | 6.6

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.

**
[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following processes provides the best historical context for the image above?
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: The image shows a group of people fighting outside of a bakery. A man is holding a loaf of bread on a pole above the crowd. Women and malnourished children are seen in the crowd.
options.caption: Source: The image is of a food riot during the famine in Ireland, a British colony at the time. It is taken from The Pictorial Times, which was originally published on October 10, 1846. Public domain.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.
 
**
[[☃ image 1]]

**The image above illustrates a contributing factor to which of the following developments in Europe between 1750 and 1900? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: The image shows a group of people fighting outside of a bakery. A man is holding a loaf of bread on a pole above the crowd. Women and malnourished children are seen in the crowd.
options.caption: Source: The image is of a food riot during the famine in Ireland, a British colony at the time. It is taken from The Pictorial Times, which was originally published on October 10, 1846. Public domain.

choices.content: urbanization and domestic migration to cities

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.**

[[☃ image 1]]

**Japanese labor in Hawai’i, shown in the image, is best understood in the context of which of the following broader processes that occurred from c.1850 to 1900? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: The image shows a field of sisal plants and four Japanese workers tending to the plants.
options.caption: Source: Japanese immigrant farmers in Hawai’i. From the Library of Congress, public domain.

choices.content: external migration to support imperial holdings

assessment_items.question_content:
**From 1750 to 1900, major cities in the Americas, Europe, and Asia grew due to migration from rural to urban areas. Which of the following factors most directly contributed to the growth of these cities? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following aspects of imperialism most directly led to increased global migration between 1800 and 1950 CE? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.**

[[☃ image 1]]

**The migration of Japanese people to North and South America in the late 1800s and early 1900s resulted in which of the following developments?  
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: The image shows a field of sisal plants and four Japanese workers tending to the plants.
options.caption: Source: Japanese immigrant farmers in Hawai’i. From the Library of Congress, public domain.

choices.content: rising anti-Asian sentiments and policies in receiving nations
the growth of trade networks across the Pacific Ocean
the spread of Buddhism to the Americas
the migration of Westerners to Japan

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the middle of the 1800s, over half of European migrants worldwide had come from the British Isles. Which of the following developments resulted in a shift in the country of origin of European migrants? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**During 1750-1900, the Indian indenture system saw over a million Indian people transported to European colonies, including the Caribbean, to work for limited or no pay for a specified period. Which of the following factors in India contributed the most to this system? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following best represents the historical context for increased immigration and indentured labor from China and India to the Caribbean between 1800 and 1900 CE? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the abolition of the slave trade
the growth of the sugar and coffee trade
increased Japanese imperialism in the region

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.
**

[[☃ image 1]]

**The policies advocated for in the poster above are most similar to which of the following policies? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A poster featuring anti-Asian sentiment, urging a 'No' vote on conscription with an illustration of a heart labeled 'Freedom' being pierced by a sword labeled 'Conscription.'"
options.caption: Source: Anti-conscription leaflet imploring voters to "keep Australia white" (1917). Public domain.

choices.content: Immigration Act of 1924 in the United States
Slavery Abolition Act of 1834 in Great Britain
Sakoku policy of isolationism in Japan
The Government of India Act of 1858

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.
**

[[☃ image 1]]

**A historian would most likely use the poster above as evidence of which of the following developments between 1850 and 1950 CE? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A poster featuring anti-Asian sentiment, urging a 'No' vote on conscription with an illustration of a heart labeled 'Freedom' being pierced by a sword labeled 'Conscription.'"
options.caption: Source: Anti-conscription leaflet imploring voters to "keep Australia white" (1917). Public domain.

choices.content: exclusion of migrants based on ideas of racial hierarchy
increased migration based on trends in colonial power
anti-war sentiment based on pacifist beliefs
quotas for migrants based on labor qualifications

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.
**

[[☃ image 1]]

**The image above is best understood in the context of which of the following developments between 1800 and 1900 CE? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A historical black-and-white photo showing a group of people, including men, women, and children, gathered in a narrow alleyway, with laundry hanging on the walls and a man in the center working over a pot.
options.caption: Source: Italian immigrants in a conventillo (tenement house), Buenos Aires, Argentina, circa 1900 CE. Public domain.
